summary: DHH celebrates Basecamp's exceptional uptime record in 2021, with all applications achieving at least 99.99% uptime and Basecamp 2 hitting five nines (99.999%). He attributes this to years of infrastructure investment including active-active dual data center deployments and redundant S3 setups across independent AWS regions. The post also notes their migration away from Google Cloud Platform after reliability issues. DHH argues that operational excellence deserves public celebration despite the superstition that bragging about uptime invites disaster.
key_points: Every Basecamp application achieved at least 99.99% uptime in 2021, with Basecamp 2 reaching five nines — their best reliability record in nearly 20 years
Active-active double data center deployments allow major upgrades with ease and instant failover when issues hit a single data center
They fully migrated off Google Cloud Platform after being burned by storage outages in 2019, replacing it with redundant multi-region S3 setups
Applications are strategically distributed across AWS regions so that a single region outage doesn't take down everything simultaneously
Operational excellence deserves celebration, not superstitious silence
money_quotes: It was a very loud year, 2021. Which makes the satisfying silence of technical incidents at Basecamp all the more of a celebration.
Basecamp 2 hit the mythical five nines with 99.999% uptime!
We finally shut down the last of our Google Cloud Platform usage, after having been burned so badly in 2019 by their file storage outages in particular.

Now I know that superstition demands that if you celebrate your uptime, you're due a calamity of failures shortly thereafter. And while I don't believe in that kind of mumbo jumbo, it totally could happen.
I don't think having a fear of celebrating the crowning achievements of technical operational excellence is wise.

Keeping the lights on for a (very broad!) set of applications with hundreds of thousands of users, petabytes of data, across on-premise and cloud, with software legacies stretching back to the dawn of this millennium? That's not easy!
key_insight: Achieving near-perfect uptime across a diverse application portfolio requires years of deliberate infrastructure investment — and that operational excellence deserves to be celebrated, not hidden behind superstition.
